  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それは戦略ボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それはウォーゲームです。",
      "それはゲームです。",
      "それはシミュレーションです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは10人の推奨プレイヤーがいます。",
      "それは推定1,500時間のプレイ時間があります。",
      "それは長さ9.5フィートのマップボードがあります。",
      "それは5人ずつの2チームに分かれた10人のプレイヤーを推奨するルールがあります。",
      "それはマップ、カウンター、小冊子、ロジスティクスシート、プラスチックカウンター収納トレイ、ダイを含むコンポーネントがあります。",
      "それは1,800個のカウンターがあります。",
      "それは6冊の小冊子があります。",
      "それは12枚のロジスティクスシートがあります。",
      "それは3つのプラスチックカウンター収納トレイがあります。",
      "それは1個の6面ダイがあります。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは史上最も複雑なウォーゲームの1つとして特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、完全なゲームを完了するのに1,500時間かかると推定される、その極端な長さによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、イタリア軍がパスタを準備するために追加の水の供給を必要とするという一般的に引用される例による複雑さによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、マップボードだけで長さ9.5フィートであることによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、5人ずつの2チームに分かれた10人のプレイヤーを推奨するルールによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、1から10のスケールで複雑さ評価が10+であることによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、各ターンがゲーム時間の1週間を表すことによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、戦闘よりもロジスティクス、補給線、正気の管理に重点を置いていることによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、巨大なゲームに対するプレイヤーのフィードバックによって特徴づけられています。",
      "それは、各ターンにすべてのユニットが燃料の蒸発で燃料の3%を失うという例のように、その難解な複雑さによって特徴づけられています。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦の全北アフリカ戦線をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それはロジスティクス、補給線、正気の管理に使用されます。",
      "それは新しいシナリオを設計するためのフレームワークとして使用されます。",
      "それは歴史の教訓として使用されます。",
      "それは純粋なシミュレーションとして使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それは第二次世界大戦の北アフリカ戦線の文脈にあります。"
    ],

      "不明"
    ],

    ],

IsA: It is a strategic board wargame.
HasA: It has ten recommended players.
It has an estimated total playtime of 1,500 hours.
It has a map board that is 9.5 ft long.
It has rules recommending ten players divided into two teams of five people.
It has components including maps, counters, booklets, logistical sheets, plastic counter storage trays, and a die.
It has 1,800 counters.
It has 6 booklets.
It has 12 logistical sheets.
It has 3 plastic counter storage trays.
It has 1 six-sided die.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being one of the most complex wargames ever published.
It is characterized by its extreme length, with a full game estimated to take 1,500 hours to complete.
It is characterized by its complexity, with a commonly cited example involving Italian troops requiring additional water supplies to prepare pasta.
It is characterized by a map board alone that is 9.5 ft long.
It is characterized by rules recommending ten players divided into two teams of five people.
It is characterized by a complexity rating of 10+ on a scale of 1-10.
It is characterized by each turn representing one week of game time.
It is characterized by being less about combat and more about managing logistics, supply lines, and sanity.
It is characterized by a development history driven by player feedback for massive games.
It is characterized by its arcane complexity, with specific examples like fuel evaporation rates differing for British units.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating the entire North African campaign of World War II.
It is used for managing logistics, supply lines, and sanity.
It is used for providing a framework for designing new scenarios.
It is used for a history lesson.
It is used for pure simulation.
LocatedIn: It is located in the context of the North African campaign of World War II.
OriginatedIn: It originated in player feedback indicating a desire for massive games.
CreatedBy: It was created by a team of developers at SPI.
It was created by Richard Berg.
It was created by Redmond A. Simonsen for cartography and graphic design.
CreatedIn: It was created in the mid-1970s.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by a team of developers at SPI.
It was developed by Richard Berg.
It was developed by Decision Games starting in 2016-2017.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in the mid-1970s.
PublishedIn: It was published under the "Heuristic Intensive Manual Simulation Series" label.
It was published by Decision Games.
WrittenBy: It was written by Al Nofi.
It was written by Richard Berg.
It was written by Jim Dunnigan.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications, Inc. (SPI).
It was produced by Decision Games.
ConsistsOf: It consists of 5 maps.
It consists of 1,800 counters.
It consists of 6 booklets.
It consists of 12 logistical sheets.
It consists of 3 plastic counter storage trays.
It consists of 1 six-sided die.
It consists of a "Historical Background" booklet.
It consists of a "Land Game Rules of Play" booklet.
It consists of an "Air and Logistics Game Rules of Play and Scenarios" booklet.
It consists of "Charts and Tables Common to Both Players".
PartOf: It is part of the "Heuristic Intensive Manual Simulation Series".
KnownFor: It is known for being one of the most complex wargames ever published.
It is known for its extreme length, with estimates of 1,500 hours to complete.
It is known for its complexity, exemplified by the pasta water requirement for Italian troops.
It is known for its massive map board.
It is known for recommending ten players.
It is known for its focus on logistics and supply lines over combat.
It is known for its development driven by player demand for monster games.
It is known for being considered unplayable by some reviewers.
It is known for its realism grade of 100% from one reviewer.
It is known for being a history lesson rather than a typical game.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with World War II.
It is associated with the North African campaign.
It is associated with Simulations Publications, Inc. (SPI).
It is associated with Richard Berg.
It is associated with Jim Dunnigan.
It is associated with Al Nofi.
It is associated with Nicholas Palmer.
It is associated with Jon Freeman.
It is associated with Decision Games.
It is associated with The Big Bang Theory.
NamedAfter: It is named after the North African campaign.
About: It is about the North African campaign of World War II.
It is about simulating the desert war.
It is about managing supply lines.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by player feedback indicating a desire for massive games.
It was influenced by the "Heuristic Intensive Manual Simulation Series" concept.
